incorrects.description: Student selected 5, confusing items per group with number of groups

params.description: All 3 boxes highlight to emphasize container count.

steps.text: You counted what's inside. The question asks how many BOXES. Count the containers, not what's in them.

incorrects.description: Student selected 8, possibly adding groups and items

steps.text: Groups means how many containers you see. Don't add. Just count the boxes.

incorrects.description: Student selected 15, counted total items instead of groups

params.description: All 3 boxes highlight to emphasize the containers.

steps.text: You counted all the markers. We need the number of GROUPS. A group is one box. How many boxes?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

steps.text: Let me show you. When we count groups, we count the CONTAINERS. Watch: 1 box, 2 boxes, 3 boxes. Three groups. The answer is 3 because there are 3 boxes.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3, confusing number of groups with items per group

params.description: First box opens to reveal 5 markers inside.

steps.text: That's how many boxes there are. Now look INSIDE one box. Count what's in there.

incorrects.description: Student selected 8, possibly adding groups and items

params.description: First box opens to reveal markers inside.

steps.text: We're not adding. Look inside ONE box. How many markers are in that box?

incorrects.description: Student selected 15, counted all items instead of items per group

params.description: First box opens, showing markers inside.

steps.text: You counted all the markers. We need how many in EACH group. Look at just one box. Count those.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: First box opens to reveal 5 markers.
Markers inside the first box highlight sequentially: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

steps.text: Here's how to find items in each group. Open one box and count what's inside. Watch: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Five markers in this box. The answer is 5 because each box contains 5 markers.
